summary: The diff implements nearly all requirements for The Daddy TGM retro magazine app. The implementation covers the 1970s visual theme, masthead, navigation, date-based issue system, seeded PRNG, content library, 50-page reader, home page, archive, soapbox, about page, and responsive design. A few specific acceptance criteria have gaps: the CSS page-turn/fade transition is implemented via a key change but lacks explicit CSS animation classes, the content library is truncated so the 200+ item count cannot be verified, and the cover page on the home page assumes the first page is always a spoofMoviePoster which may not always be true. Additionally, some boilerplate ICP/Internet Identity code was included that was explicitly listed as a non-goal.

missing_requirements.requirement: A visible CSS transition (fade or slide) occurs when changing pages
missing_requirements.reason: The reader uses a React key change (animKey) to trigger re-renders but no explicit CSS fade/slide animation class or keyframe is visible in the truncated index.css. Cannot confirm a page-turn or fade CSS animation is defined and applied to the page wrapper.

missing_requirements.requirement: At least 200 distinct content items are defined across all categories combined; each category has at least 20+ unique entries
missing_requirements.reason: The contentLibrary.ts file is truncated and only shows the beginning of spoofMoviePosters. It is impossible to verify from the diff that 200+ items exist across all 6 categories with 20+ each.

missing_requirements.requirement: A cover-style spoof movie poster is shown as the featured cover (seeded)
missing_requirements.reason: HomePage.tsx uses pages[0] as the cover and conditionally renders only if contentType === 'spoofMoviePoster', but the issue generator does not guarantee page 1 is always a movie poster since it shuffles a mixed pool. The cover may not display for all seeds.

hallucinated_features.description: Internet Identity authentication infrastructure including useInternetIdentity hook, useActor hook, InternetIdentityProvider, and AuthClient integration
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request explicitly states 'No real user authentication or accounts' and 'No backend canister logic' as non-goals. Yet a full Internet Identity auth system with AuthClient, identity management, and actor creation was implemented and wrapped around the entire app.
The build request explicitly lists 'No backend canister logic — this is a fully frontend-driven app' as a non-goal, yet a backend/main.mo file was created.
